Customer Support.description: Covers ALL queries from users who have already purchased something, created an account, or have an active transactional relationship with a business or e-commerce platform. The defining trait is that money has changed hands or an account exists. CHOOSE THIS DOMAIN for: tracking or cancelling orders, requesting refunds or returns, reporting damaged/wrong items, resolving login or account access problems, fixing failed payments, requesting invoices, and asking about product details before buying. KEY SIGNALS: 'my order', 'my account', 'I bought', 'I purchased', 'order #', 'delivery', 'package', 'refund', 'return', 'can't log in', 'payment failed', 'card declined', 'invoice', 'receipt', 'is this in stock', 'how much does X cost', 'wrong item', 'damaged'. DO NOT CHOOSE THIS for: investment or tax questions (→ Finance), general coding help (→ Coding), medical symptoms (→ Healthcare), or study help (→ Education). EDGE CASES: 'How much does the pro plan cost?' → Customer Support (product pricing). 'My package didn't arrive' → Customer Support (order tracking). 'I received a broken item' → Customer Support (return/refund).
examples: Where is my order #12345?
I want to return this item, it's damaged
I can't log in to my account
My payment failed at checkout
Is the blue version of this product in stock?

Orders.description: All queries about orders that have already been placed and exist in the system — the user has committed to a purchase and is now inquiring about, tracking, cancelling, or modifying that specific order. CHOOSE THIS when there is an existing order involved. KEY SIGNALS: 'my order', 'order #', 'order status', 'I ordered', 'package', 'shipment', 'delivery', 'hasn't arrived', 'where is my', 'cancel my order', 'change my address', 'wrong item in my order', 'modify my order'. DO NOT CHOOSE THIS for: products the user hasn't purchased yet (→ Product Queries), refund/return requests after delivery (→ Refunds & Returns), payment failures (→ Payments). EDGE CASES: 'My package is late' → Orders/Track Order. 'I want to cancel before shipping' → Orders/Cancel Order. 'Wrong size delivered' → could be Initiate Return if delivered, or Modify Order if not yet shipped.
examples: Where is my package?
Cancel my order immediately
I put the wrong address — can I change it?
My order hasn't arrived and it's been 2 weeks
Order status for #XYZ123

Track Order.description: The user wants to know the current location, shipping status, or estimated arrival time of an order they already placed. They are NOT trying to cancel or change the order — they just want to know WHERE IT IS and WHEN IT WILL ARRIVE. This is a pure information/status request. SIGNALS: 'where is my order', 'where is my package', 'track my order', 'when will it arrive', 'when will it be delivered', 'has it shipped yet', 'tracking number', 'estimated delivery date', 'out for delivery', 'in transit', 'USPS/FedEx/UPS/DHL update', 'delivery status', 'order not arrived', 'package delayed', 'shipment update', 'expected delivery', 'still not here'. DISTINGUISH FROM Cancel Order: Cancel Order involves terminating the order; Track Order is passive information. DISTINGUISH FROM Return Status: Return Status is about a RETURN already sent back; Track Order is about a FORWARD shipment to the customer.
examples: Where is my package right now?
When will my order arrive?
Has order #12345 shipped yet?
Can you give me the tracking link for my order?
My delivery was supposed to arrive today, where is it?
What's the current status of my shipment?

Cancel Order.description: The user explicitly wants to STOP, ABORT, or REVERSE a placed order before it is fulfilled or delivered. This is a decisive ACTION request — the user no longer wants the item. SIGNALS: 'cancel my order', 'I want to cancel', 'stop my order', 'I changed my mind', 'don't want this anymore', 'cancel order #XYZ', 'please cancel', 'I don't want it', 'undo my purchase', 'don't ship it', 'stop the delivery', 'abort the order', 'I made a mistake, cancel it'. DISTINGUISH FROM Modify Order: Modify is a partial change; Cancel is full termination. DISTINGUISH FROM Initiate Return: Return is AFTER delivery; Cancel is BEFORE delivery. If the user says both 'where is it AND I want to cancel', prefer Cancel Order — the action intent is primary.
examples: I want to cancel my order #789
Please cancel — I changed my mind
Can I still cancel this? It hasn't shipped yet
Stop my order immediately, I don't want it
I ordered the wrong thing, cancel it
I want to undo my purchase

Modify Order.description: The user wants to CHANGE A SPECIFIC DETAIL of an existing, not-yet-shipped order without cancelling it entirely. They want to keep the order but fix something about it. Changes include: delivery address, item size/color/variant, quantity, shipping speed upgrade, or adding/removing items from the order. SIGNALS: 'change my order', 'update my address', 'wrong address on order', 'can I change the size', 'different color', 'add an item', 'remove an item', 'different quantity', 'upgrade to express shipping', 'edit my order', 'fix my order', 'I made a mistake on my order'. DISTINGUISH FROM Cancel Order: Modify keeps the order alive with changes; Cancel terminates it. DISTINGUISH FROM Track Order: Track is passive information; Modify is an active change request.
examples: I put the wrong address on my order, please update it
Can I change the size from M to XL?
Update delivery address for order #321 to my new address
I ordered the wrong color, can I switch it to black?
Can I add one more item to my order?
Upgrade my shipping to next-day please

Refunds & Returns.description: Queries about reversing a purchase AFTER the product has been received — getting money back, physically sending the product back, or checking on an already-started return. The product has typically been delivered. CHOOSE THIS when the user has received something and wants to undo the purchase or check on an existing return. KEY SIGNALS: 'return', 'refund', 'send back', 'exchange', 'wrong item received', 'damaged product', 'money back', 'I got the wrong thing', 'broken on arrival', 'defective', 'return label', 'where is my refund', 'return status', 'has my return been processed'. DO NOT CHOOSE THIS for: orders not yet delivered (→ Orders), payment disputes about charges (→ Payments). EDGE CASES: 'I got a damaged item' → Initiate Return. 'I returned it last week, where's my money?' → Return Status.
examples: I want to return this broken item
When will I get my refund?
How do I send this back to you?
I got a damaged item, I want a full refund
Status of my return — I sent it back 5 days ago

Initiate Return.description: The user wants to START the return or refund process for a product they have received. They have NOT yet begun the process — this is the very first step. The product arrived but they are unhappy with it for some reason (damaged, wrong item, changed mind, not as described). SIGNALS: 'I want to return', 'how do I get a refund', 'start a return', 'return request', 'I want my money back', 'this is not what I ordered', 'wrong item', 'damaged on arrival', 'defective product', 'I want to exchange', 'return policy', 'can I get a refund for', 'item arrived broken', 'this is not as described'. DISTINGUISH FROM Return Status: Return Status is for checking an ALREADY-STARTED return. This is the beginning of a new return.
examples: I want to return this product, it's not what I expected
I received a damaged item, how do I get a refund?
How do I start a return for my order?
This isn't what I ordered — I want my money back
I'd like to exchange this for a different size
Can I get a refund for my purchase? I'm not happy with it

Return Status.description: The user has ALREADY INITIATED a return and wants to know its current status — whether it has been received at the warehouse, when the refund will be credited to their account, or what stage the return process is at. The return process is underway and they are following up. SIGNALS: 'status of my return', 'when will I get my refund', 'has my return been processed', 'I already sent it back', 'return #', 'where is my refund', 'refund timeline', 'how long does the refund take', 'refund still not received', 'my return was accepted, where is the credit', 'I returned it X days ago'. DISTINGUISH FROM Initiate Return: Initiate is STARTING a new return. This is CHECKING on an existing one.
examples: Where is my refund? I returned the item 5 days ago
Has my return been received at the warehouse?
Status of return #456 — when do I get my money?
My refund is taking too long, it's been 2 weeks
I sent the item back last Monday, what's the status?
How long will my return take to process?

Return Item.description: The user needs practical, LOGISTICAL instructions on HOW TO PHYSICALLY SEND an item back — packaging requirements, return label, drop-off locations, courier options, prepaid shipping. They may already have a return approved but need the step-by-step of physically getting the item back to the seller. SIGNALS: 'how do I ship this back', 'return label', 'drop off location for return', 'how to pack the item for return', 'return shipping', 'prepaid label', 'where do I drop off', 'return instructions', 'what courier to use', 'can I get a return shipping label'. DISTINGUISH FROM Initiate Return: Initiate Return is about triggering the process in the system. This is about the PHYSICAL act of returning the product.
examples: How do I ship this item back to you?
Where can I drop off my return package?
Can you send me a return shipping label?
How should I pack the item for return?
Which courier should I use to return this?
Do I need the original box to return this product?

Account Issues.description: Problems or requests related to the user's personal ACCOUNT on the platform — authentication failures, password problems, account lockouts, or updating stored personal information. The issue is about the ACCOUNT ITSELF, not a specific order or product. CHOOSE THIS when the user cannot access their account or needs to update their profile data. KEY SIGNALS: 'can't log in', 'account locked', 'forgot password', 'reset password', 'change my email', 'update my phone', 'my account is suspended', 'login not working', '2FA problem', 'update my profile', 'wrong email on my account', 'account compromised', 'security issue'. DO NOT CHOOSE THIS for: payment problems on a specific transaction (→ Payments), order-related issues (→ Orders).
examples: I can't log in to my account
I forgot my password, send a reset link
Update my email address on my account
My account appears to be locked
Change my phone number on file

Login Help.description: The user CANNOT ACCESS their account due to authentication failures, account lockouts, unrecognized credentials, two-factor authentication (2FA) issues, or a blocked/suspended account. They are stuck outside their account. SIGNALS: 'can't log in', 'account locked', 'login not working', 'incorrect password error', 'says wrong password', 'account blocked', 'account suspended', 'not letting me in', '2FA not working', 'verification code not received', 'sign in issue', 'access denied', 'forgot my username', 'can't get into my account', 'keeps logging me out'. DISTINGUISH FROM Reset Password: Reset Password is when the user knows they specifically need a password reset. Login Help is broader — the user may not know why they can't get in.
examples: I can't log in to my account — it says invalid credentials
My account appears to be locked
2FA code isn't working and I can't log in
I keep getting 'access denied' when trying to sign in
I'm locked out of my account, how do I get back in?
My account has been suspended for no reason

Reset Password.description: The user explicitly wants to RESET, RECOVER, or CHANGE their password. They have identified the password as the specific problem. SIGNALS: 'forgot my password', 'reset my password', 'password reset link', 'change my password', 'password expired', 'I need a new password', 'I think my account was hacked — change password', 'password recovery', 'can't remember my password', 'send me a reset link', 'new password setup'. DISTINGUISH FROM Login Help: Login Help is when the cause of access failure is unclear. Reset Password is specifically about the password. Even if they say 'can't log in, need to reset password', classify as Reset Password since the action is explicit.
examples: I forgot my password, how do I reset it?
Please send me a password reset link
How do I change my password?
I want to set a new password for my account
My password expired, how do I create a new one?
I think my account was hacked — help me reset my password

Update Account.description: The user wants to CHANGE STORED PERSONAL INFORMATION in their account profile — email address, phone number, delivery address, name, date of birth, notification preferences, or linked payment methods. They are already logged in (or just want the data changed) and need to edit their profile details. SIGNALS: 'update my profile', 'change my email', 'new phone number', 'update my address', 'change my name on account', 'edit my account details', 'update billing address', 'change notification settings', 'remove my saved card', 'update my personal information'. DISTINGUISH FROM Login Help / Reset Password: Those are about accessing the account. Update Account is about editing information once access is established.
examples: I want to change my email address on my account
Update my phone number to my new number
Change my default delivery address
Edit my profile name — there's a typo
Update my billing address
I moved, please update my address on file

Payments.description: Issues and inquiries directly related to FINANCIAL TRANSACTIONS on the platform — failed charges, unexpected or disputed charges, billing cycles, and transaction documentation. CHOOSE THIS when the core issue is about money MOVING (or failing to move) between the user and the platform. KEY SIGNALS: 'payment failed', 'payment declined', 'card declined', 'charge', 'billed', 'invoice', 'receipt', 'billing', 'transaction declined', 'overcharged', 'double charged', 'why was I charged', 'subscription billing', 'payment error', 'checkout failing'. DO NOT CHOOSE THIS for: product pricing before purchase (→ Product Queries), refunds for returned items (→ Refunds & Returns), or bank account management unrelated to the platform (→ Finance > Banking). EDGE CASES: 'My card was declined at checkout' → Payment Failed. 'I was charged twice for one order' → Billing Inquiry.
examples: My payment didn't go through at checkout
I was charged twice for the same order
I need an invoice for my purchase for expense reporting
Why was I billed $49 this month?
My credit card is being declined on your site

Payment Failed.description: A payment attempt was DECLINED, FAILED, or DID NOT COMPLETE. The user tried to pay and it did not go through — they want to understand why and how to fix it so they can complete their purchase. SIGNALS: 'payment failed', 'payment declined', 'transaction failed', 'could not charge my card', 'payment not going through', 'checkout keeps failing', 'card declined', 'payment error', 'order not placed due to payment issue', 'unable to process payment', 'my card is being rejected', 'payment not accepted'. DISTINGUISH FROM Billing Inquiry: Billing Inquiry is about a charge that DID go through (but is questioned). Payment Failed is about a charge that NEVER went through.
examples: My payment failed when I tried to check out
My card was declined — why?
Payment keeps failing, I can't complete my order
Transaction failed on your site
I tried three different cards and none of them work
Payment error — unable to process my order

Billing Inquiry.description: The user has a question or dispute about an EXISTING CHARGE — they see something on their statement they don't understand, think they were overcharged, or want to know what a charge is for. The payment DID go through but something is wrong or confusing about it. SIGNALS: 'what is this charge', 'I was overcharged', 'unexpected charge', 'billing question', 'subscription charge', 'why was I billed', 'double charged', 'charged twice', 'billing cycle', 'I was charged but didn't order', 'mystery charge from your company', 'I don't recognize this payment'. DISTINGUISH FROM Payment Failed: The charge DID go through here. DISTINGUISH FROM Invoice Request: User isn't disputing anything, just needs a document.
examples: Why was I charged $49 this month? I didn't order anything
I was billed twice for the same order
There's an unexpected charge on my account
I was overcharged — the price was supposed to be $20 not $30
When is my next subscription billing date?
I don't recognize a charge from your company on my card

Invoice Request.description: The user needs a FORMAL RECEIPT, INVOICE, or PROOF-OF-PAYMENT DOCUMENT for a transaction — typically for expense reporting, tax purposes, business reimbursement, or accounting records. The transaction was successful; they just need the paper trail. SIGNALS: 'need an invoice', 'send me a receipt', 'invoice for my order', 'proof of purchase', 'tax invoice', 'official receipt', 'GST/VAT invoice', 'business expense receipt', 'download my invoice', 'invoice for accounting', 'need documentation of this payment'. DISTINGUISH FROM Billing Inquiry: User isn't disputing anything — they just need the document.
examples: I need an invoice for order #123 for my expense report
Please send me a receipt for my recent purchase
I need proof of purchase for my employer reimbursement
Can I get a GST invoice for this transaction?
Email me the official invoice for order #456
I need formal documentation of this payment for tax purposes

Product Queries.description: Requests for information to help a user make a PURCHASING DECISION — details about a product they are considering buying but have NOT YET ORDERED. The user is in pre-purchase research mode. CHOOSE THIS when the user wants to know about a product BEFORE buying it. KEY SIGNALS: 'what does X do', 'specs of', 'is X compatible with', 'does it come in', 'how much is X', 'is X available', 'what is included', 'is it waterproof/wireless/etc', 'does it support', 'which version should I buy', 'is this in stock'. DO NOT CHOOSE THIS for: products already ordered (→ Orders), general educational questions about a topic (→ Education). EDGE CASES: 'How much does the Pro plan cost?' → Pricing Info. 'Is the 256GB version available?' → Check Availability.
examples: What are the full specs for this laptop?
Is this product available in size 10?
How much does the premium plan cost per month?
Is this backpack waterproof?
Is the blue color variant in stock?

Product Details.description: User wants specific FACTUAL INFORMATION about a product — its specifications, materials, dimensions, compatibility, features, or how it works — to make a purchase decision. SIGNALS: 'what are the specs', 'dimensions of', 'is it compatible with', 'what material is it made of', 'does it have X feature', 'how does it work', 'technical specifications', 'what's included in the box', 'is it waterproof', 'wireless or wired', 'battery life', 'weight of', 'what operating system does it run', 'does it support X'. DISTINGUISH FROM Pricing Info: This is about features, not cost. DISTINGUISH FROM Check Availability: This is about what the product is like, not whether it can be purchased.
examples: What are the specifications of this laptop?
Is this Bluetooth headphone compatible with iPhone 15?
What material is this backpack made of?
Does this device support fast charging?
What's included in the box when I order this?
Is this phone waterproof — what's the IP rating?

Check Availability.description: User wants to know if a specific product is CURRENTLY IN STOCK, available in their region, or available in a particular variant (size, color, model, storage). SIGNALS: 'is this in stock', 'available in my area', 'when will it be back in stock', 'do you have size X', 'available in blue', 'out of stock', 'can I get this in', 'stock check', 'availability', 'is there stock', 'when can I buy this', 'sold out', 'do you ship to X country', 'available in XL'. DISTINGUISH FROM Product Details: This is purely about whether/when it can be purchased, not what it is like.
examples: Is this sneaker available in size 11?
Do you have this jacket in black?
Is this product currently in stock?
When will this item be back in stock?
Can I get this delivered to Canada?
Is the 512GB version available?

Pricing Info.description: User wants to know the CURRENT PRICE, applicable discounts, sale price, or cost breakdown for a product or service BEFORE buying. SIGNALS: 'how much does X cost', 'what's the price of', 'is there a discount', 'current sale price', 'pricing for', 'how much is shipping', 'price comparison', 'is this on sale', 'coupon or promo code', 'price drop notification', 'total cost with tax', 'subscription cost per month', 'annual vs monthly price'. DISTINGUISH FROM Product Details: This is about cost, not features. DISTINGUISH FROM Billing Inquiry: A charge already happened in Billing Inquiry; this is pre-purchase research.
examples: How much does this laptop cost?
Is there a discount code for this product?
What's the monthly price of the Pro plan?
How much is express shipping to New York?
Is this item currently on sale?
What's the total price including tax for this order?

Travel.description: Covers queries where the user ACTUALLY WANTS TO TRAVEL or plan travel — flights, accommodation, trip planning, and travel documentation. The defining trait is PHYSICAL MOVEMENT from one place to another. CHOOSE THIS DOMAIN for: searching or booking flights/hotels, trip itineraries, destination recommendations, visa requirements, and passport information. KEY SIGNALS: 'flight', 'hotel', 'trip', 'travel', 'itinerary', 'destination', 'visa', 'passport', 'vacation', 'book a room', 'fly to', 'journey', 'accommodation', 'airline', 'airport', 'layover', 'check-in', 'resort', 'hostel', 'airfare', 'travel plan'. ★ CRITICAL EXCLUSION: If the user asks for CODE TO DO SOMETHING travel-related → Coding, NOT Travel. 'I want code to book a flight' → Coding. 'Write a script to find cheap hotels' → Coding. 'API to search flights' → Coding. ONLY choose Travel if the user THEMSELVES wants to travel or get travel info — not build software about travel. DO NOT CHOOSE THIS for: package delivery tracking (→ Customer Support > Orders), coding involving travel APIs (→ Coding). EDGE CASES: 'Book me a flight to Paris' → Travel (person wants to fly). 'Code to book a flight on Indigo' → Coding (person wants software). 'How do I integrate the Skyscanner API?' → Coding.
examples: Book me the cheapest flight to London
Best hotels in Paris under $150/night
Plan a 7-day Europe trip for me
Do I need a visa for Japan as a US citizen?
Is my flight BA142 on time?

Booking > Flights.description: Queries about searching for, comparing, booking, or getting information about specific FLIGHTS. The focus is on AIR TRAVEL specifically. CHOOSE THIS when the user is asking about flights: prices, routes, timings, direct vs connecting, or the status of a specific flight. KEY SIGNALS: 'flight', 'airline', 'fly to', 'airfare', 'flight number', 'departure time', 'landing', 'non-stop', 'direct flight', 'economy class', 'business class', 'cheapest flight', 'flight status', 'is my flight on time'. DO NOT CHOOSE THIS for: hotels (→ Booking > Hotels), ground transport, or general trip planning (→ Planning).
examples: Cheapest flight from NYC to Tokyo
Next direct flight to London from Chicago
Is flight AI202 delayed today?
Flights under $400 to Miami this weekend
What time does the next flight to Sydney depart?

Cheapest Flight.description: User wants to find the MOST AFFORDABLE flight option to a destination — they are prioritizing low cost over speed, comfort, or convenience. Budget is the primary filter. SIGNALS: 'cheapest flight', 'cheapest way to fly', 'budget flight', 'lowest airfare', 'most affordable flight', 'cheap tickets', 'lowest price flight', 'economy deals', 'budget airline', 'discount flight', 'best price for flights to X', 'flight deals', 'cheap flights', 'least expensive flight'. DISTINGUISH FROM Fastest Flight: Fastest prioritizes speed/directness; Cheapest prioritizes cost. DISTINGUISH FROM Flight Status: Status is about an existing booked flight.
examples: What's the cheapest flight from NYC to Paris?
Cheapest tickets to Bali this December
Budget flight options from London to Dubai
Find the lowest airfare to Bangkok
Cheapest economy class seats to Sydney
Best flight deals from Toronto to Mexico City

Fastest Flight.description: User wants the QUICKEST or most DIRECT flight — fewest layovers, shortest total travel time, or earliest arrival. Speed and convenience are the primary criteria. SIGNALS: 'fastest flight', 'direct flight', 'non-stop flight', 'quickest route', 'shortest travel time', 'fewest layovers', 'earliest arrival', 'next available flight', 'earliest flight tomorrow', 'one-stop max', 'fastest way to get to X', 'direct route', 'no layover'. DISTINGUISH FROM Cheapest Flight: Cheapest prioritizes cost; Fastest prioritizes speed. The user here doesn't care as much about price.
examples: What's the fastest flight from London to Tokyo?
Any direct non-stop flights from NYC to LA?
Non-stop flight to Dubai from Boston?
Quickest way to get from Sydney to Singapore
Earliest morning flight tomorrow to Chicago
Is there a direct flight with no layovers to Singapore?

Flight Status.description: User wants the REAL-TIME or CURRENT STATUS of a SPECIFIC, ALREADY-IDENTIFIED flight — whether it is on time, delayed, cancelled, or has landed. The flight exists and has a flight number or scheduled departure. SIGNALS: 'flight status', 'is my flight on time', 'flight delayed', 'flight cancelled', 'flight [number]', 'arrival or departure status', 'gate information', 'has the plane landed', 'flight tracker', 'is flight X delayed', 'check my flight', 'flight update'. DISTINGUISH FROM Cheapest/Fastest: Those are SEARCHING for a future flight. Flight Status is CHECKING on an already known/booked flight.
examples: Is flight BA142 on time right now?
My flight is showing delayed — what's the latest?
Has flight AA101 from JFK landed yet?
What gate is my flight departing from?
Is IndiGo flight 6E204 cancelled today?
Check the current status of flight EK500

Booking > Hotels.description: Queries about finding, comparing, or booking HOTEL ACCOMMODATION or lodging. CHOOSE THIS when the user's need is specifically about WHERE TO STAY — not flights, trip planning, or documentation. KEY SIGNALS: 'hotel', 'room', 'accommodation', 'stay', 'book a room', 'check-in', 'check-out', 'resort', 'hostel', 'motel', 'Airbnb-style', 'overnight', 'bed and breakfast', 'suite', 'hotel availability', 'hotel near'. DO NOT CHOOSE THIS for: flights (→ Booking > Flights), full trip planning (→ Planning), or travel documents (→ Documentation).
examples: Best budget hotel in Rome under $80/night
5-star luxury resort in Paris for honeymoon
Is the Marriott Times Square available next weekend?
Cheap hostels in Bangkok near the city center
Book a hotel room in Tokyo for 3 nights in April

Budget Hotel.description: User is looking for AFFORDABLE, LOW-COST, or VALUE-FOR-MONEY accommodation. Cost is the primary filter — they want the cheapest comfortable place to stay. SIGNALS: 'cheap hotel', 'budget hotel', 'affordable accommodation', 'inexpensive hotel', 'hostel', 'under $X per night', 'cheapest hotel', 'budget stay', 'economical option', 'value hotel', 'low-cost accommodation', 'cheap place to stay', 'budget-friendly hotel'. DISTINGUISH FROM Luxury Hotel: Luxury prioritizes quality/prestige; Budget prioritizes cost.
examples: Cheapest hotel in Barcelona near the city center
Budget accommodation in Bali under $30 a night
Best hostels in Amsterdam for backpackers
Affordable hotel near the airport in London
Cheapest place to stay in Tokyo for a week
Budget-friendly hotels in New York under $100

Luxury Hotel.description: User wants HIGH-END, PREMIUM, or UPSCALE accommodation — 4 or 5-star hotels, luxury resorts, suites, or boutique experiences. Quality and experience are the primary criteria. SIGNALS: '5-star hotel', 'luxury hotel', 'premium accommodation', 'luxury resort', 'best hotel in', 'penthouse suite', 'spa hotel', 'five-star', 'finest hotel', 'high-end stay', 'VIP accommodation', 'top-rated hotel', 'world-class hotel', 'boutique hotel', 'overwater bungalow'. DISTINGUISH FROM Budget Hotel: Budget minimizes cost; Luxury maximizes quality and prestige.
examples: Best 5-star hotel in Paris for a special occasion
Luxury beachfront resort in the Maldives
Book an overwater bungalow in Bora Bora
Top-rated luxury hotels in Dubai
Premium hotel with spa in Singapore for a honeymoon
Best boutique hotel in Rome for a romantic getaway

Hotel Availability.description: User wants to know if a SPECIFIC HOTEL (already identified) has rooms available on particular dates — they have a hotel in mind and just need to check inventory. SIGNALS: 'is [hotel name] available', 'rooms available on dates', 'available this weekend', 'check availability at [hotel]', 'any rooms left', 'fully booked', 'vacancy at', 'can I get a room at X on Y dates', 'book [specific hotel]', 'room for 2 adults', 'available December 20-25'. DISTINGUISH FROM Budget/Luxury Hotel: Those involve FINDING a hotel type. This is checking a specific, already-known property.
examples: Is the Hilton Midtown available next weekend?
Check room availability at Marriott for December 15-18
Any vacancies at the Grand Hyatt on Friday night?
Is the Four Seasons Bangkok available in January?
Rooms available for 2 adults at Hotel X on March 5th?
Is this hotel fully booked for New Year's Eve?

Planning.description: Queries about PLANNING A TRIP at a higher, strategic level — designing the overall journey: what to do, where to go, how to structure time, what to pack, and how much the trip will cost. The user is in inspiration or planning mode, not transaction mode. CHOOSE THIS when the user is DESIGNING a trip experience rather than booking a specific component. KEY SIGNALS: 'plan my trip', 'itinerary', 'what to do in', 'recommend a destination', 'travel tips', 'trip budget', 'best places to visit', 'where should I travel', 'things to see in', 'travel guide', 'day by day plan', 'trip schedule'. DO NOT CHOOSE THIS for: booking a specific flight (→ Booking > Flights) or specific hotel (→ Booking > Hotels).
examples: Plan a 10-day Japan trip for me with a day-by-day schedule
What are the must-see things to do in Rome?
Best destinations for a beach vacation in December
How much does a 2-week trip to Europe cost?
Recommend the best places to visit in Southeast Asia

Itinerary Planning.description: User wants a STRUCTURED, ORGANIZED, TIME-BOUND PLAN for a trip — a day-by-day or hour-by-hour schedule of activities, transport, meals, and sightseeing for a specific duration. SIGNALS: 'plan my trip to X', 'day-by-day itinerary', 'travel plan', 'what should I do in X for Y days', 'schedule for my trip', '3-day plan for Paris', 'organize my vacation', 'trip schedule', '7-day itinerary', 'plan out my days in X'. DISTINGUISH FROM Travel Recommendations: Recommendations are open-ended suggestions; Itinerary is a structured time-bound plan. DISTINGUISH FROM Budget Planning: Budget focuses on cost; Itinerary focuses on activities and time.
examples: Create a 7-day day-by-day itinerary for Japan

Coding.description: ★ FORM-FIRST DOMAIN: If the user is asking for CODE TO BE WRITTEN or wants help with CODE THEY HAVE — classify as Coding regardless of the subject matter. 'I want code to book a flight' → Coding. 'Write a script to check my bank balance' → Coding. 'Function to calculate drug dosage' → Coding. The subject (travel, finance, health) does NOT override the fact that CODE is being requested. CHOOSE THIS DOMAIN for: writing new code (functions, scripts, APIs, programs), debugging existing broken code (errors, exceptions, crashes), and designing software architectures. KEY SIGNALS: 'I want code', 'write code', 'write a function', 'write a script', 'code to do X', 'generate code', 'help me code', 'implement', 'debug this', 'error in my code', 'exception', 'bug in my code', 'fix my code', 'why is my code failing', 'design a system', 'architecture for', 'class diagram', 'database schema', specific programming languages (Python, JavaScript, Java, Go, C++, SQL, TypeScript, Rust), error messages, stack traces, 'API integration', 'refactor', 'script to automate'. KEY DISTINCTION from Education: 'Write me a Python function to sort a list' → Coding (CREATE code). 'Explain how sorting algorithms work' → Education (LEARN concept). KEY DISTINCTION from Travel: 'I want code to book a flight on Indigo' → Coding (wants CODE). 'Book me a flight to Paris' → Travel (wants to TRAVEL).

Write me a Python function to sort a list by the second element
Design the high-level architecture for a food delivery app
My JavaScript Promise isn't resolving, what's wrong?
Help me integrate the Stripe payment API into my Node.js app

Debugging.description: The user has EXISTING CODE that is BROKEN, producing ERRORS, or BEHAVING UNEXPECTEDLY — they need help identifying the root cause and fixing it. The code already exists; the problem is something wrong with it. CHOOSE THIS when the user presents code with errors, exceptions, or unexpected behavior. KEY SIGNALS: error message text, 'error', 'exception', 'bug', 'not working', 'crash', 'broken', 'undefined', 'null pointer', 'TypeError', 'SyntaxError', 'it's throwing', 'getting an error', 'my code fails', 'unexpected output', 'code not working', 'why does this return', 'can't figure out this bug'. DO NOT CHOOSE THIS for: writing new code from scratch (→ Code Generation), architecture planning (→ System Design). EDGE CASES: If the user says 'fix this code' AND it clearly has a bug/error → Debugging. If they say 'write me new code' → Code Generation.
examples: Getting a TypeError in my Python script — line 42
Why is my JavaScript function returning undefined?
My Java application keeps crashing with a NullPointerException
SyntaxError in my React component I can't figure out
My Python script is throwing a KeyError on this dict

Debug Python.description: User has a BUG, ERROR, or UNEXPECTED BEHAVIOR specifically in PYTHON code and needs help diagnosing and fixing it. SIGNALS: Python-specific errors (TypeError, ValueError, KeyError, AttributeError, IndentationError, ImportError, ModuleNotFoundError, NameError), Python keywords and syntax (def, class, import, pip, conda, virtualenv), Python frameworks (Django, Flask, FastAPI, pandas, NumPy, scikit-learn, SQLAlchemy), '.py files', 'Python script', 'Python function', 'my Python code'. DISTINGUISH FROM Debug JavaScript: Different language. DISTINGUISH FROM Runtime Error: Use Debug Python for any clearly Python error, regardless of whether it's a compile or runtime error.

My Django app is throwing a 500 Internal Server Error
Python AttributeError: NoneType object has no attribute 'split'
Pandas dataframe .groupby() not returning expected results
ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'requests' in Python
IndentationError in my Python function — where is the mistake?

Debug JavaScript.description: User has a BUG, ERROR, or UNEXPECTED BEHAVIOR specifically in JAVASCRIPT, TYPESCRIPT, or a JS framework and needs help fixing it. SIGNALS: JS-specific errors (ReferenceError, undefined is not a function, Cannot read properties of undefined, Promise rejection, CORS error, TypeError in JS), JavaScript/TypeScript keywords (const, let, async/await, Promise, .then/.catch, require, import/export, arrow functions), frameworks (React, Next.js, Vue, Angular, Express, Node.js, Svelte), '.js/.ts/.tsx files', 'npm error', 'yarn error', 'webpack', 'Vite'. DISTINGUISH FROM Debug Python: Different language. Use this for ALL JavaScript and TypeScript bugs.

My async/await code isn't resolving — what am I doing wrong?
Unhandled Promise rejection in Node.js
Why is my useState hook not updating the component?
Node.js ECONNREFUSED when trying to connect to database
My Vue.js computed property is not reactive

DISTINGUISH FROM Debug Python: Use Debug Python for Python errors. DISTINGUISH FROM Debug JavaScript: Use Debug JavaScript for JS/TS errors. Use Runtime Error for all other languages or when the language isn't identified.

Segmentation fault in my C++ program when parsing input

Program crashes after processing about 1000 requests

Block Card.description: User wants to FREEZE, BLOCK, DISABLE, or REPORT their debit or credit card — typically due to suspected fraud, theft, or loss. This is an urgent security action. SIGNALS: 'block my card', 'freeze my card', 'lost my card', 'stolen card', 'suspicious transaction — block card', 'disable my card', 'report card stolen', 'emergency block', 'card compromised', 'someone used my card fraudulently', 'cancel my card', 'deactivate my card'. DISTINGUISH FROM Check Balance: Block Card is a security action; Check Balance is information only.
examples: Block my credit card immediately — I think it was stolen
I lost my debit card, freeze it right now
My card was stolen, disable all transactions
There's a suspicious transaction — block my card now
Cancel my debit card and issue a new one
I need to freeze my credit card until I find it

Transaction History.description: User wants to VIEW, RETRIEVE, UNDERSTAND, or DISPUTE specific PAST TRANSACTIONS in their bank account. SIGNALS: 'transaction history', 'account statement', 'recent transactions', 'list of charges', 'what did I spend', 'download statement', 'past payments', 'I don't recognize a transaction', 'last 30 days activity', 'show me my spending', 'transaction details', 'bank statement', 'account activity'. DISTINGUISH FROM Check Balance: Balance is the current total; Transaction History is the detailed record of past activity.
examples: Show me my last 10 bank transactions
Download my full bank statement for March
I don't recognize a transaction from 2 days ago
List all my spending from last month
What did I spend on food this week?
Get me the statement for my savings account for Q1

Investments.description: Queries about GROWING WEALTH through financial markets — stocks, mutual funds, bonds, ETFs, and personal portfolio management. CHOOSE THIS when the user is thinking about investing, managing their portfolio, or researching financial instruments. KEY SIGNALS: 'stock', 'shares', 'portfolio', 'mutual fund', 'ETF', 'market', 'invest', 'returns', 'dividend', 'S&P 500', 'index fund', 'NASDAQ', 'market cap', 'P/E ratio', 'buy stocks', 'investment returns', 'my holdings'. DO NOT CHOOSE THIS for: everyday banking (→ Banking), tax on investments (→ Taxes), or cryptocurrency as a general topic.
examples: How is Apple stock performing today?
Should I invest in index funds or individual stocks?
Review my investment portfolio allocation
What's the Tesla stock price right now?
Is my portfolio well-diversified?

Stock Query.description: User wants INFORMATION ABOUT A SPECIFIC STOCK, company shares, ETF, or financial instrument — its current price, historical performance, market news, analyst ratings, or investment outlook. SIGNALS: 'stock price of X', 'share price', 'Tesla/Apple/NVIDIA/Amazon stock', 'is X stock a good buy', 'stock performance', 'market cap of X', 'dividend yield', 'stock up/down today', 'P/E ratio', 'stock analysis', 'should I buy X stock', 'market trend', 'index fund performance'. DISTINGUISH FROM Portfolio Review: Portfolio Review is about the USER'S OWN holdings; Stock Query is about a specific external instrument.
examples: What's the current Apple stock price?
How has Tesla stock performed this year?
Is NVIDIA stock a good buy right now?
Amazon stock analysis — is it overvalued?
S&P 500 year-to-date performance
What's the dividend yield on Microsoft stock?

Portfolio Review.description: User wants an OVERVIEW, ANALYSIS, or ASSESSMENT of their PERSONAL INVESTMENT PORTFOLIO — total value, asset allocation, performance, diversification, or advice on rebalancing. SIGNALS: 'my portfolio', 'portfolio performance', 'review my investments', 'how is my portfolio doing', 'asset allocation', 'should I rebalance', 'portfolio diversification', 'my holdings', 'total investment value', 'investment summary', 'my net worth from investments', 'is my portfolio balanced'. DISTINGUISH FROM Stock Query: Stock Query is about a specific stock; Portfolio Review is about the USER'S personal collection of investments.
examples: How is my investment portfolio performing this quarter?
Review my stock holdings and tell me if they're balanced
Is my portfolio well-diversified or too concentrated?
Should I rebalance my portfolio — I'm too heavy in tech
What's my total return on investments this year?
My asset allocation breakdown — am I too risky?

Taxes.description: Queries related to TAX OBLIGATIONS, TAX FILING PROCEDURES, or TAX REDUCTION STRATEGIES. CHOOSE THIS when the question is specifically about taxes — whether income, capital gains, self-employment, or business taxes. KEY SIGNALS: 'tax', 'IRS', 'HMRC', 'tax return', 'file taxes', 'tax deduction', 'tax refund', 'write off', 'tax exempt', 'W-2', '1099', 'income tax', 'tax bracket', 'self-employed tax', 'capital gains tax', 'tax deadline'. DO NOT CHOOSE THIS for: billing disputes on a specific platform (→ Customer Support > Payments) or investment questions not involving tax (→ Investments).
examples: How do I file my tax return this year?
What expenses can I deduct as a freelancer?
Tax filing deadline for self-employed individuals
Can I deduct my home office for remote work?
First time filing taxes — complete guide

Tax Filing.description: User needs help with the PROCESS or MECHANICS of SUBMITTING A TAX RETURN — deadlines, which forms to use, what documents to gather, how to e-file, or understanding the overall filing process. SIGNALS: 'how to file taxes', 'tax filing deadline', 'file my return', 'ITR filing', 'tax forms', 'do I need to file', 'tax software', 'e-file', 'first time filing taxes', 'self-employed tax filing', 'W-2 form', '1099 filing', 'how to submit my tax return'. DISTINGUISH FROM Tax Deductions: Deductions are about WHAT TO CLAIM; Filing is about the ACT and PROCESS of submitting.
examples: How do I file my federal tax return step by step?
What's the tax filing deadline for 2024?
First time filing taxes as a freelancer — where do I start?
Best tax software to use — TurboTax vs H&R Block?
Do I need to file taxes if I earned less than $12,000?
How do I e-file my income tax return online?

Tax Deductions.description: User wants to understand what EXPENSES, CREDITS, or DEDUCTIONS they are eligible to CLAIM to reduce their tax liability. The focus is on WHAT TO WRITE OFF or claim. SIGNALS: 'tax deductions', 'can I deduct X', 'what can I write off', 'tax exemptions', 'tax credits', 'business expense deduction', 'home office deduction', 'how to reduce my taxes', 'capital gains exemption', 'freelancer deductions', 'eligible deductions', 'maximize my refund'. DISTINGUISH FROM Tax Filing: Filing is about the submission process; Deductions are about minimizing what you owe.
examples: What can I write off as a self-employed freelancer?
Can I deduct home office expenses if I work from home?
Tax deductions available for work-from-home employees
What tax credits are available for first-time homebuyers?
Can I deduct my car if I use it for business?
How do I maximize my tax refund this year?

Education.description: Queries focused on LEARNING and ACADEMIC ACTIVITIES — understanding concepts, preparing for exams, completing academic work, and building knowledge. The defining trait is INTELLECTUAL LEARNING rather than professional task execution. CHOOSE THIS DOMAIN for: explaining concepts (science, math, history, programming concepts), requesting tutorials or how-to guides, exam preparation, practice questions, homework help, and project assistance. KEY SIGNALS: 'explain', 'how does X work', 'what is X', 'help me understand', 'exam prep', 'homework', 'assignment', 'study', 'tutorial', 'learning', 'teach me', 'I don't understand'. KEY DISTINCTION FROM CODING: 'Explain how recursion works' → Education (understanding concept). 'Write a recursive function for me' → Coding (create code). 'What is an API?' → Education. 'Call this API for me' → Coding. DO NOT CHOOSE THIS for: actually writing production code (→ Coding), medical advice (→ Healthcare), or product research (→ Customer Support).
examples: Explain how recursion works to me
How should I prepare for the GRE exam?
Help me solve this calculus homework problem
Give me practice questions for the biology exam
How does photosynthesis work?

Learning.description: Requests where the user wants to UNDERSTAND SOMETHING — gain knowledge about a concept, theory, process, or skill through explanation or step-by-step instruction. The output is UNDERSTANDING, not a deliverable or code. CHOOSE THIS when the user is in LEARNING MODE. KEY SIGNALS: 'explain', 'what is', 'how does X work', 'I don't understand', 'teach me', 'tutorial', 'guide to', 'walk me through', 'what's the difference between', 'how do I learn', 'beginner guide to'. DISTINGUISH FROM CODING: If they want CODE WRITTEN → Coding. If they want to UNDERSTAND a concept → Education/Learning. DO NOT CHOOSE THIS for: exam preparation (→ Exams) or specific homework/project completion (→ Assignments).
examples: Explain how the internet works at a high level
What is machine learning and how does it work?
How do I use Python decorators?
Walk me through how Git branching works
I don't understand how neural networks learn

Concept Explanation.description: User wants a CLEAR EXPLANATION of a specific CONCEPT, TERM, THEORY, or PHENOMENON — to build understanding of the idea itself. No step-by-step guide needed — they want the 'what' and 'why'. SIGNALS: 'what is X', 'explain X', 'how does X work', 'what does X mean', 'concept of X', 'define X', 'I don't understand X', 'what's the difference between X and Y', 'ELI5 X', 'explain like I'm 5', 'what is meant by X'. DISTINGUISH FROM Tutorial Request: Tutorial is a step-by-step HOW-TO guide; Concept Explanation is about understanding the IDEA itself.
examples: What is recursion and why is it useful?
Explain how TCP/IP networking works
What's the difference between machine learning and deep learning?
How does compound interest actually work?
Explain the concept of supply and demand with examples
What is quantum computing in simple terms?

Tutorial Request.description: User wants STEP-BY-STEP INSTRUCTIONS or a HANDS-ON GUIDE to DO or LEARN something — a walkthrough they can follow to accomplish a task or learn a skill. SIGNALS: 'how to do X', 'tutorial for X', 'step-by-step guide', 'walk me through', 'how to set up X', 'beginner guide to X', 'show me how to', 'guide to getting started', 'how do I get started with X', 'complete guide to X'. DISTINGUISH FROM Concept Explanation: Concept is understanding an idea; Tutorial is following a procedure to achieve a result. NOTE: 'How do I use Python decorators?' → Tutorial (step-by-step how-to). 'What are decorators in Python?' → Concept Explanation.
examples: How to set up a React project from scratch — step by step
Step-by-step guide to start investing in stocks
Tutorial for learning Python as a complete beginner
How to create a pivot table in Excel — walk me through it
Step-by-step guide to starting a blog
Complete beginner's guide to meditation practice

Exams.description: Queries specifically about EXAM-RELATED ACTIVITIES — preparing for a test, study strategies, or practicing with questions. The context is explicit academic or professional testing. CHOOSE THIS when an exam, test, or certification is mentioned or strongly implied. KEY SIGNALS: 'exam', 'test', 'GRE', 'GMAT', 'SAT', 'UPSC', 'board exam', 'certification exam', 'study for', 'practice questions', 'mock test', 'how to prepare for exam', 'test prep'. DO NOT CHOOSE THIS for: general concept learning without an exam context (→ Learning) or homework/project completion (→ Assignments).
examples: How should I prepare for the GRE verbal section?
Give me 10 practice questions for calculus
Study plan for the UPSC civil services exam
Mock test for Java Spring certification
Tips for scoring 700+ on the GMAT

Exam Preparation.description: User wants STRATEGIES, STUDY PLANS, TIMELINES, RESOURCES, or TIPS for preparing for an upcoming exam — the 'how to study' approach. SIGNALS: 'how to prepare for X exam', 'study plan for X', 'tips for cracking X', 'strategy for GRE/GMAT/SAT', 'preparation for board exams', 'how to study for finals', 'what to focus on', 'study schedule', 'best resources for X exam', 'how many months to prepare', 'exam preparation guide'. DISTINGUISH FROM Practice Questions: Practice Questions provides actual test material; Exam Preparation is about the preparation STRATEGY.
examples: How should I prepare for the GRE in 3 months?
Create a 6-month study plan for the UPSC exam
Best tips and strategies for cracking the CAT exam
How many months do I need to prepare for the GMAT?
How to study for finals in 1 week effectively?
Best resources and books to prepare for the AWS Solutions Architect exam

Practice Questions.description: User wants ACTUAL SAMPLE QUESTIONS, MOCK TESTS, QUIZZES, or PRACTICE PROBLEMS to test and reinforce their knowledge. They want material to PRACTICE WITH, not just strategies. SIGNALS: 'practice questions for X', 'mock test', 'sample problems', 'quiz me on X', 'past year papers', 'test my knowledge', 'practice MCQs', 'give me some problems to solve', 'flashcards for X', 'sample exam questions', 'practice paper'. DISTINGUISH FROM Exam Preparation: Exam Prep is strategy; Practice Questions is actual test content the user practices with.
examples: Give me 5 calculus integration practice questions
Mock test for GRE verbal reasoning
Practice MCQs for biology cell division chapter
Sample problems for data structures and algorithms
Quiz me on the periodic table elements
Past year UPSC questions on modern Indian history

Assignments.description: Requests for help COMPLETING specific ACADEMIC DELIVERABLES — homework problems, essays, research papers, or project work that has been assigned to the user. The user has a specific task they need to finish and submit. CHOOSE THIS when there is a specific academic task with a deadline or submission requirement. KEY SIGNALS: 'homework', 'assignment', 'project', 'essay', 'term paper', 'due tomorrow', 'my teacher asked', 'for class', 'school project', 'coursework', 'help me complete', 'capstone project'. DO NOT CHOOSE THIS for: general learning (→ Learning) or exam preparation strategies (→ Exams).
examples: Help me solve this differential equations homework problem
I need to write a 1000-word essay about climate change for class
Help me with question 5 from my physics assignment
Science fair project ideas for 8th grade
Help me complete my semester capstone project on AI

Homework Help.description: User needs help SOLVING, UNDERSTANDING, or COMPLETING a specific, DISCRETE HOMEWORK PROBLEM or SHORT ASSIGNMENT — typically a well-defined question or problem from a homework set. SIGNALS: 'help with my homework', 'solve this problem', 'homework question', 'I don't understand this problem', 'math homework', 'physics problem', 'chemistry question', 'answer this question from my assignment', 'help me solve this', 'homework due tomorrow'. DISTINGUISH FROM Project Help: Homework is a specific problem or short task; Project Help is for larger, multi-part academic work.
examples: Help me solve this differential equation: dy/dx = 2x
I don't understand question 5 on my chemistry homework
Help me balance this chemical equation for class
Solve this probability problem from my math homework
Answer this history question about World War II for my assignment
Help me with my statistics homework — hypothesis testing question

Project Help.description: User needs help with a LARGER, MULTI-STAGE ACADEMIC PROJECT — research papers, essays, reports, or sustained pieces of academic work that require planning and execution. SIGNALS: 'my school project', 'term paper', 'research project', 'capstone', 'help with my essay', 'science fair project', 'project on X', 'write a report about', 'design a project', 'semester project', 'research paper', 'thesis help'. DISTINGUISH FROM Homework Help: Homework is specific and small; Project Help is broader, multi-part, and typically requires more research and structure.
examples: Help me write a 5-page research paper on climate change
Science fair project ideas for a 10th-grade student
Help me plan and structure my semester capstone project on AI ethics
I need to build a design portfolio for my art class
Essay on the causes of the French Revolution — help me outline and write it
Help me complete my capstone project on machine learning applications

General.description: A LAST-RESORT SAFETY NET domain for queries that GENUINELY DO NOT FIT any other domain, are too vague to classify even at low confidence, or contain multiple fully conflicting intents spanning different domains. CHOOSE THIS DOMAIN ONLY WHEN NO OTHER DOMAIN FITS — even at 0.3 confidence, prefer a real domain over General. Do NOT default to General for vague queries if ANY domain can be inferred. Do NOT choose General for queries that are slightly unusual or complex — push hard to find the best-fitting domain first. KEY SIGNALS FOR GENERAL: purely social greetings with zero content ('Hello'), completely unrelated topics forced together ('debug my code AND book a hotel AND file my taxes'), nonsensical or gibberish text, or queries so vague that even with imagination you cannot place them in a domain.
examples: Hello
I need help with something
Cancel my flight AND also debug my Python code
What is the meaning of life?
Can you assist me with my issue?

Miscellaneous.description: The catch-all category within General for queries that are completely unrecognizable, have no clear domain, are inherently contradictory multi-domain, or are purely social with no actionable content. USE VERY SPARINGLY — try all other domains first.
examples: Hello, can you help?
I have a problem but I don't know what kind
Book a hotel AND fix my code AND file my taxes
What should I do today?

Unknown Query.description: The query is COMPLETELY UNRECOGNIZABLE, NONSENSICAL, or maps to NO topic in the system whatsoever. The LLM cannot determine ANY domain even at 0.3 confidence. SIGNALS: gibberish text, random words with no meaning, non-sequiturs with absolutely zero context, queries in completely unsupported topics with no overlap to any domain. NOTE: Use this EXTREMELY sparingly — only if there is truly zero domain match.
examples: asdfghjkl
purple banana xkcd 42
What is the color of Tuesday?
Translate this to Klingon: blargon flurp

Ambiguous Query.description: The query has SOME CONTENT but is too VAGUE, BRIEF, or CONTEXT-FREE to classify confidently into any specific domain or intent. More information would be needed. SIGNALS: 'help', 'I have a problem', 'I need assistance', 'can you help me with something', extremely generic one-word or two-word queries with absolutely no context. NOTE: Prefer a low-confidence classification in a real domain over Ambiguous. Only use if the query is so minimal that it truly could mean anything.
examples: Help
I need help with something
Can you assist me?
I have a question
I'm not sure what to do

Multi-intent Query.description: The query EXPLICITLY contains 2 or more FULLY DISTINCT requests spanning COMPLETELY DIFFERENT DOMAINS — it cannot be resolved to a single primary intent. SIGNALS: explicit connectors like 'AND ALSO', 'AND', combining unrelated domains in one query like 'book a flight AND debug my code', 'reset my password AND explain quantum physics'. NOTE: Only use this when the query truly cannot be resolved to a single dominant intent. If one intent is clearly primary and the other is incidental or background, classify by the primary.
examples: Book a flight to Paris and also help me debug my Python code
Cancel my Amazon order and explain how compound interest works
Fix my JavaScript bug AND plan my Tokyo trip itinerary
Reset my bank password AND help me file my taxes
